purpose: Compact course context for future PS369 question-authoring sessions. Read this before raw CourseMaterials to minimize token use.
read_order: CourseMaterials/PS369_StudyGuides/plain_text/*.txt (canonical topic-coverage list — every concept/person/event named there should have a question)
CourseMaterials/PS369_LectureMaterials/plain_text/*.txt (31 lecture .docx/.pptx extracted to text — grep here to verify 'Course lectures' sources)
CourseMaterials/*.docx / *.pptx — raw originals, only if the plain-text extract is insufficient

extraction_tooling.behavior: Idempotent re-runnable extractor. Converts every .docx/.pptx/.pdf under PS369_Handouts/, PS369_LectureMaterials/, PS369_StudyGuides/, plus the standalone syllabus docx, into plain_text/*.txt mirrors. Re-run after adding new materials.

study_guides.how_to_use: These list JGM's canonical Key Concepts and People/Places/Events split pre-midterm (lectures 1-9) and post-midterm (lectures 10-17). Every named item should have at least one question in the bank. Use as the primary gap-coverage checklist when expanding.

lecture_materials.how_to_use: Grep-verify 'Course lectures' source attributions here. If the correct-answer fact does not appear in any plain-text lecture file, do not label the source as 'Course lectures' — cite a specific reading or a verifiable URL instead. See content/batch4_source_audit.md for the worked example.

course.name: PS369 Post-Soviet Politics: Russia, Ukraine, and the Road to War

documents.title: Explaining the Collapse of the Soviet System
best_for: disaggregating political, economic, and federal collapse

documents.title: Key Historical Events
best_for: Kyivan Rus and competing successor narratives

documents.title: Political Institutions
best_for: PR, SMD, and mixed electoral systems

bank_snapshot.rebalancing_note: 2000s and 2010s_plus are the thinnest eras and should be the focus of future expansion. Study guides' post-midterm concept list (Tandemocracy, Putinism, Putinomics, Hybrid warfare, Khodorkovsky Affair, Chechenization, Beslan, Moscow apartment bombings, Minsk II, Zelensky's Servant of the People, etc.) is the canonical gap checklist.

game_mode_no_repeat_threshold.reason: Bank passed 300 questions at 374. Switch game mode to non-repeating by default on next touch of Game.tsx / gameConfig.ts.
game_mode_no_repeat_threshold.future_rule: Non-repeating game mode should be the default now; re-examine only if the bank shrinks or difficulty distribution becomes unusable.

question_model.soviet_collapse_handling: Soviet-collapse content is distributed across the 3 categories and surfaced through the `soviet` era filter rather than a standalone category.

authoring_rules: Read this file first before re-opening raw CourseMaterials.
Use plain-text extracts under `CourseMaterials/**/plain_text/` (handouts, lectures, study guides) + the syllabus plain-text extract before reopening raw DOCX/PPTX.
Use raw CourseMaterials only for fact-checks, exact reading attribution, or disputed wording.

Study guides in `PS369_StudyGuides/plain_text/` are JGM's canonical topic list — every concept, person, place, and event named there should have at least one question in the bank. Treat them as the default gap checklist.

Before labeling any question's source as 'Course lectures', grep-verify the correct-answer fact in `PS369_LectureMaterials/plain_text/*.txt`. If the fact goes beyond lecture coverage, cite a specific reading or verifiable URL instead. 'General post-Soviet politics literature' is not an acceptable source label.
Named-author or specific-argument questions should usually be gated with `era_filter_visibility` based on the syllabus pre-/post-midterm split.
Write prompts as direct factual questions rather than saying 'according to the course' or using similar course-meta framing.
In economic questions, prefer the specific phrase 'economic diversification' when that is the concept being tested.
Do not over-flag serious topics for sensitivity when the wording is clinical and text-only.
For hard and very_hard questions, distractors must be logically consistent and plausible enough that the answer cannot be guessed through absurd elimination.

feedback_system.target: Question-level feedback modal is live in quiz and game screens. Reports post to the configured Google Apps Script / Google Sheet endpoint, with local-device queue fallback if delivery fails or the endpoint is unavailable.
feedback_system.special_rule: Feedback containing `*JGM*` is tagged as `auto_apply_next_pass` at submission time. The deployed Apps Script now supports token-protected `doGet` readback, so future sessions can pull those items back in and apply them as long as the user provides the private token out of band. Other feedback stays in the review queue.

game_filters.target: Game mode now opens with a filter screen so difficulty, era, and country constrain the question pool before level play begins.

quiz_batches.target: Quiz mode now runs in batches of 20 questions and offers continue / redo missed / return home choices between batches.
